name: Tempeh Tacos with Breaded and Baked Cauliflower

ingredients.item: plus 2 tablespoons unsweetened dairy-free milk, divided

method: Cut the tempeh widthwise into ¼-inch strips, then tear those into about ½-inch pieces.
Mix 1 tablespoon hot sauce and 2 tablespoons milk together in a small bowl. Add tempeh and toss. Let marinate for 1 hour.
Meanwhile, prepare the cauliflower by cutting the florets into small bite-size pieces.
Mix 2 tablespoons of hot sauce and ¼ cup milk in a large bowl. Add the cauliflower and toss.
Add the flour, taco seasoning, panko, and nutritional yeast to a large bowl and mix well.

Take the cauliflower out of the wet mixture and add to the flour mixture and toss to coat all of the florets. Place on a baking sheet and bake for 20 to 30 minutes, turning after 15 minutes. You will be able to pierce them easily with a fork when done.

While the cauliflower is baking, heat the oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat and add the tempeh. Cook for about 2 minutes, flipping occasionally, until the pieces become golden brown. Remove from the heat to a paper towel.
Assemble the taco by laying some lettuce in the bottom of a shell and spooning in some tempeh, cauliflower, and tomato. Serve with lime wedges and salsa, if desired.
